title: QA Loxodromes — T-operator constant-bearing paths on mod-24 lattice
_fixture_purpose: Certifies that QA T-operator paths are discrete loxodromes (constant-bearing). Three orbit types demonstrated: cosmos (period 24), satellite (period 8), singularity (period 1). Bearing spread = e²/G. Mercator identity s_φ = tanh²(ψ) verified at 5 latitudes. Each orbit type represents a class of rhumb lines on the discrete lattice.
_derivation: A loxodrome = constant-bearing path. On a sphere, bearing is fixed → path spirals toward a pole. On a QA mod-m lattice, the T-operator IS the constant generator → path cycles after π(m) steps. The bearing of this discrete loxodrome is determined by the direction (d,e): bearing_spread = sin²(angle_from_north) = e²/(d²+e²) = e²/G. The Mercator projection maps loxodromes to straight lines; in spread coordinates, s_φ = tanh²(ψ) where ψ = isometric (Mercator) latitude.

witnesses.label: Cosmos loxodrome: (1,1) mod 24
witnesses._note: Full 24-step circumnavigation. Bearing spread 1/2 = 45° from north (NE). Fibonacci sequence mod 24.

witnesses.label: Cosmos loxodrome: (3,5) mod 24
witnesses._note: Same 24-step period, different starting point. Bearing spread 144/169 ≈ 67.4° from north.

witnesses.label: Satellite loxodrome: (8,8) mod 24
witnesses._note: Reduced 8-step loxodrome. The 8 principal compass winds. Satellite orbit = loxodrome that doesn't circumnavigate.

witnesses.label: Singularity: (24,24) mod 24
witnesses._note: Degenerate loxodrome — no motion. Fixed point. The navigator at the pole: all bearings converge.

mercator_identity.label: Cape Town
mercator_identity._note: Southern hemisphere: negative ψ. tanh²(-0.6296) = 0.3111 = sin²(33.9°)

mercator_identity.label: High latitude (75°)
mercator_identity._note: Near pole: ψ grows large. tanh²(2.028) = 0.9330 = sin²(75°)

qa_connection.t_operator_is_constant_bearing: The T-operator is a single fixed generator applied repeatedly. This IS a constant-bearing path — the discrete analogue of a loxodrome. The generator doesn't change; only the state advances.
qa_connection.pisano_is_circumnavigation: The Pisano period π(m) is the number of steps to complete the loxodrome cycle. π(24)=24 for cosmos orbits. The loxodrome spirals and returns after exactly π(m) steps — impossible on a continuous sphere (continuous loxodromes never close, except for cardinal bearings).
qa_connection.orbit_partitions_loxodromes: Cosmos = full circumnavigation rhumb lines (24 steps). Satellite = reduced rhumb lines (8 steps, the principal winds). Singularity = degenerate (no motion). This partition is STRUCTURAL — it arises from the algebra, not from geometry.
qa_connection.mercator_is_spread: The Mercator identity s_φ = tanh²(ψ) shows that spreads ARE Mercator-native quantities. The isometric latitude ψ is the natural coordinate for loxodromes; its square hyperbolic tangent IS the spread. Mercator projection was a spread projection all along.
qa_connection.theorem_nt: Continuous latitude φ → spread s_φ is observer projection. The QA loxodrome operates on discrete states (b,e) mod m. Mercator coordinate ψ is a second observer projection. The discrete path is the reality; the projected charts are views.

sources: Loxodrome: Pedro Nunes (1537), first description of rhumb lines
Pisano period: Wall (1960), QA T-operator periodicity
Isometric latitude: Bowditch, American Practical Navigator
